ops.text:
PRE-REGISTRATION E30.3 stage 0 (judge-vs-auditor agreement audit) + E30.4 (standing calibration axes). Both $0, no model calls, read-only on all sources; independent VERIFY recompute lanes gate both.

E30.3 stage 0 — Data: auditor-gold v1, all 541 rows (descriptive audit, no optimization/selection; test rows untouched by any tuning). Judge side = canonical latest_judge verdict (triage pv6) per the mint; auditor side = blind opus re-judgment. Method, computed once: Cohen's κ on binary is_steering (PRIMARY) and on 11-way category (secondary), overall + stratified by auditor_prompt_version (3/4/5), labeler model, and fidelity; the gpt-5.5-labeled stratum quarantined per E20 (reported separately, excluded from the primary κ); 95% bootstrap CI (10k, seed=303) on the primary κ. Pre-registered ladder on primary κ point estimate: ≥0.75 → single-judge design stands for Wave 0; 0.5–0.75 → Wave 0 experiments that judge with a single model upgrade to PoLL 3-jury; <0.5 → triage-judge task redesign flag raised to the user before Wave 0 judging. Stage 1 (Rating Roulette, $8–15) triggers ONLY if the CI spans a ladder boundary.

E30.4 — Deliverable: standing calibration utilities (~/.cc-steer/experiments/E30/e30_4/calib.py: ECE 10 equal-mass bins mirroring cc_steer/retrain/lexical.py ece(), Brier, AURC/risk-coverage, split-conformal for the pick task) + a baseline calibration report for (i) the incumbent watcher's stored probs on the frozen n=628 (fullframe_scores.npz) and (ii) E29's FT pick probs on n=202 (conformal calibrated on a train slice, never the eval slice). Numbers are DESCRIPTIVE baselines for later tie-breaks — pre-registered rule: 'calibration win' may only ever break ties between arms whose primary deltas are both non-actionable; it never promotes on its own.
